page: Google Cloud Platform c4n-standard-24 is an x86_64 architecture server instance featuring 24 dedicated virtual CPUs and 90 GB of system memory. This configuration yields a resource density of 3.75 GB of RAM per vCPU core. The instance does not include integrated graphics processing units or local storage drives, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached storage options. Additionally, it does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Operating with dedicated CPU allocation, the c4n-standard-24 provides predictable compute performance without resource sharing. The absence of bundled local storage and GPUs makes this instance a streamlined option for workloads that do not require hardware acceleration or high-speed local scratch space. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the resource profile is balanced for workloads requiring a moderate memory-to-core ratio, avoiding the premium costs associated with memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware classes.

This server configuration is suited for general-purpose workloads, multi-threaded applications, web serving, and medium-scale database hosting. The 24 dedicated vCPUs support parallel processing tasks, while the 90 GB of RAM accommodates standard application runtimes, containerized microservices, and continuous integration pipelines that do not demand extreme memory footprints.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4n-standard-24 is an x86_64 architecture server instance configured with 24 dedicated vCPUs and 90 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 3.75 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not feature local storage or integrated GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage for data persistence. With its dedicated CPU allocation, the c4n-standard-24 delivers consistent compute performance without the overhead of shared-core resource contention. Qualitatively, this configuration offers a balanced resource profile, making it a cost-efficient choice for workloads that require multi-core processing capabilities without the premium associated with high-memory or GPU-accelerated instances. It is well-suited for general-purpose applications, web serving, containerized deployments, and moderate database workloads.

tagline: An x86_64 compute instance featuring twenty-four dedicated virtual cores and ninety gigabytes of memory for balanced multi-threaded workloads.
